items.title: Intel's upcoming 'Raptor Lake Next' will reportedly top out at 20 cores and retain Core 200 branding — Lineup may include a special 10-core SKU with 24MB of L3 cache
items.description: Intel's Raptor Lake family might be coming back for a third time and sit alongside Nova Lake on shelves as the budget-oriented offering from the company.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 7 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Yesterday, we broke news of a third Raptor Lake refresh in the works from Intel, dubbed "Raptor Lake Next." Following that, new leaks have emerged from reliable tipster Jaykihn confirming the codename and detailing the upcoming family. Raptor Lake Next is slated for early 2027 and will land on the LGA 1700 socket as reported previously, but some outlets say it will also retain the existing Core 200 branding.- Raptor Lake silicon, not Bartlett Lake- Early next year- Desktop and mobile (125W, 65W; HX)- Coexists with 14th generation availability- No fresh features from RPL-R. For comparison, RPL-R had many from RPL, such as WiFi 7, Fast Throttle, APO, etc.Specs soon.June 13, 2026According to the rumors, Raptor Lake Next doesn't use Bartlett Lake silicon, which is Intel's special lineup for edge and embedded devices with only P-cores. That means a 12 P-core SKU has been ruled out for now, and that Raptor Lake Next will stick to the usual hybrid config. It's expected to scale across mobile and desktop with up to 125W parts and HX models as well, but it won't bring any new notable features.Essentially, we're looking at rebranded 14th Gen CPUs that are likely being re-released because of their high yields. Intel spent a considerable amount of time and effort fixing the stability issues with 13th and 14th Gen families, so it makes sense the company wants to maximize its returns here. The Core-i9 14900K is still the most performant gaming CPU Intel has ever released, so calling this leftover silicon doesn't feel right either.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Jaykihn also provided further specs for the Raptor Lake Next, claiming it will only spread across Core 3, Core 5, and Core 7; no Core 9 SKUs in sight at the moment. At the top sits a Core 7 part with 8 P-cores and 12 E-cores, totaling out to 20 cores rated at 65W, same as the Core i7-14700. Then there's a 16-core 125W processor, which is identical to the Core i7-13700K, but it's counted as "Core 5" for Raptor Lake Next.Raptor Lake Next Desktop iGPU Enabled SKUsC7 8+12 65WC5 8+8 125WC5 6+4 65W 24MB L3C3 4+0 65WThe i5 6+4 65W 24MB L3 is a new configuration (Default 20MB L3 for 6+4).The closest alternatives are the 13490f/14490f, which do not have integrated graphics enabled. https://t.co/Q5nzFtSMTvJune 14, 2026There's a special 10-core 65W part with 24MB of L3 cache because the previous 10-core silicon only had 20MB. It looks like the cache from disabled core clusters will still be accessible in certain Raptor Lake Next SKUs, and this might become a trend going forward. Lastly, we have a Core 3 CPU with 4 P-cores and no E-cores, running at 65W as well. Since all these chips are based on Raptor Lake silicon, they use Raptor Cove P-cores and Gracemont E-cores. You may like Intel reportedly preparing surprise return to DDR4 systems with 'Raptor Lake Next' Intel will reportedly upgrade to an 8-core config for its Wildcat Lake refresh next year Intel reportedly no longer working on 6-core Nova Lake mobile SKU, alleges new rumor Keep in mind that RTL and RTL-R both came with Intel HD 700 series integrated graphics; the new Arc Xe architecture debuted with Arrow Lake on desktop. Nonetheless, Raptor Lake Next will work with DDR4 memory because it uses the LGA 1700 platform. That could help alleviate at least some of the burden of building a PC today as DDR5 RAM doesn't seem to be getting cheaper anytime soon.Raptor Lake Next is apparently going into production in January 2027 so it could be available in Q1 2027, coexisting with Nova Lake as perhaps the value-focused option from the Blue Team. All rumors point toward Nova Lake being delayed to next year, but officially we still expect an announcement later this year. Regardless, the two families sure look like they'll overlap, which is why the Core 200 vs Core 400 distinction would be important for consumers.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 英特尔即将推出的“Raptor Lake Next”据报道将最高配备20核心，并沿用Core 200系列品牌——产品线可能包含一款特殊的10核SKU，配备24MB L3缓存
items.descriptionZh:
Intel计划在2027年初推出“Raptor Lake Next”，这是其第13/14代酷睿（Raptor Lake）架构的第三次刷新版本，将沿用LGA 1700接口，并继续使用现有的“Core 200”系列品牌命名。此消息由可靠爆料人Jaykihn证实，继昨日我们报道了Intel正在开发第三版Raptor Lake的消息后进一步披露了更多细节。

**背景与上下文**：Raptor Lake Next并非基于此前为边缘和嵌入式设备设计、仅配备P核的Bartlett Lake芯片，而是沿用标准的大小核混合架构（Raptor Cove P核 + Gracemont E核）。这意味着此前传闻的12个P核顶级型号已被排除。该系列将覆盖台式机和移动平台，包括最高125W功耗的SKU和HX系列，但不会引入任何新特性——相比之下，上一代RPL-R（Raptor Lake Refresh）曾带来WiFi 7、Fast Throttle、APO等新功能。本质上，Raptor Lake Next是对第14代酷睿的重命名，很可能是因为Intel在花费大量时间精力修复13/14代稳定性问题后，希望利用高良率芯片最大化回报。Core i9-14900K仍然是Intel史上最强游戏处理器，因此称之为“剩余芯片”并不恰当。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：技术上没有创新，它是基于成熟Raptor Lake硅片的再发布。所有芯片均使用Raptor Cove P核和Gracemont E核，集成Intel HD 700系列显卡（而非Arrow Lake上的全新Arc Xe架构）。值得注意的是，由于采用LGA 1700平台，Raptor Lake Next将支持DDR4内存，在当前DDR5价格居高不下的背景下，这有助于降低整机搭建成本。另外，部分SKU的L3缓存有所增加：例如10核型号（6P+4E，65W）配备了24MB L3缓存，而此前同配置仅有20MB；这表明某些型号中，从禁用核心簇中释放的缓存仍可被利用，可能成为未来趋势。

**性能参数与对比数据**：Jaykihn提供了详细规格，Raptor Lake Next仅覆盖Core 3、Core 5和Core 7系列，目前无Core 9型号。顶级型号为Core 7：8个P核+12个E核，共20核，65W功耗，与Core i7-14700相同。次旗舰为Core 5：8个P核+8个E核，共16核，125W功耗，与Core i7-13700K相同。此外还有一款特殊的Core 5：6个P核+4个E核，65W，24MB L3缓存（此前类似配置如13490F/14490F的L3为20MB，且无集成显卡）。最低端为Core 3：4个P核，无E核，65W。所有型号均集成显卡。

**技术影响与应用场景**：Raptor Lake Next计划于2027年1月进入量产，预计2027年第一季度上市。它将与Intel下一代架构Nova Lake共存，作为面向预算市场的“蓝色阵营价值选择”。考虑到Nova Lake可能延期至2027年之后，两个家族将出现明显重叠，此时Core 200与Core 400的命名区分对消费者至关重要。对于计划升级的PC用户，Raptor Lake Next提供了一个成熟、稳定且兼容DDR4的性价比平台，尤其适合游戏和主流办公场景，但要注意它不包含任何新架构特性（如Arrow Lake的改进能效或新GPU）。整体而言，这是Intel在过渡期的务实战略，通过延长成熟架构生命周期来平衡研发投入和市场需求。

items.title: Grab this MSI Codex Z2 16-thread gaming Ryzen PC with a 2TB SSD at a $400 discount — system packs a Ryzen 8700F, 16GB DDR5, and RTX 5060 Ti 8GB for $1,499
items.description: MSI's prebuilt gaming desktop pairs a Zen 4 processor with Nvidia's latest RTX 5060 Ti graphics card and comes housed in an airflow-focused chassis with ARGB lighting.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Building a gaming PC can be an overwhelming task, especially if it's your first one, as the sheer number of choices and technical specifications can make the process feel intimidating. That's where prebuilts come to the rescue, and today we have a solid mid-range offering from MSI that is currently on sale at Newegg. Originally priced at $1,899, the MSI Codex Z2 A8NVM-485US is down to $1,499, offering savings of $400. Newegg is also offering special bundles with this prebuilt PC, including a 32-inch 4K monitor, keyboard, mouse, wireless headset, webcam, and even a UPS, helping you complete your next battlestation. Check out this deal on Newegg The MSI Codex Z2 comes with a compact airflow-focused chassis with a large mesh panel at the front with triple 120mm ARGB fans as well as a glass side panel to showcase the internals. The PC is powered by an AMD Ryzen 7 8700F, a powerful Zen 4 processor featuring 8 cores, 16 threads, and an AI-accelerating NPU. It is capable of hitting up to 5 GHz boost clocks, however, it does not come with an integrated graphics solution. For graphics you get the Nvidia RTX 5060 Ti 8GB offering all the latest tech including DLSS 4.5 with multi-frame generation, improved ray tracing performance, and more. Save 21% ($400)MSI Codex Z2 A8NVM-485US : was $1,899 now $1,499 at NeweggThe MSI Condex Z2 powered by the Ryzen 7 8700F and RTX 5060 Ti is currently selling at $1,499 saving you $400 and the hassle of choosing the right parts and assembling them. View Deal The rest of the features include 16GB of DDR5-5200 RAM, a 2TB M.2 NVMe Gen4 SSD to store all your games, and a 650W 80 Plus Gold power supply which should offer enough juice to power all the hardware. The CPU is cooled using a single-tower air cooler and in terms of wireless connectivity the system comes with built-in Wi-Fi 6E and Bluetooth 5.3. I/O options include a USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A, a USB 3.2 Gen 2x2 Type-C, along with dedicated headphone and mic ports at the front. At the rear you get two USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A, two USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-A, four USB 2.0 Type-A, audio ports including optical, gigabit Ethernet, and even a PS/2 port. If you have been considering a gaming PC upgrade from an older generation, the MSI Codex Z2 is worth a look at its discounted price of $1,499. The combination of a Ryzen 7 8700F, RTX 5060 Ti, and 16GB of DDR5 memory should provide plenty of horsepower for modern games at 1080p and some older titles in 1440p resolution. If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, gaming chair, or CPU Deals pages.
items.author: Kunal Khullar
items.titleZh: 抓住这个微星Codex Z2 16线程游戏Ryzen电脑，配备2TB固态硬盘，直降400美元——系统搭载Ryzen 8700F、16GB DDR5和RTX 5060 Ti 8GB，售价1,499美元
items.descriptionZh:
MSI Codex Z2 A8NVM-485US 游戏主机降价促销：搭载 Ryzen 7 8700F 与 RTX 5060 Ti，售价降至 1,499 美元

对于初次组装游戏 PC 的玩家而言，面对琳琅满目的硬件选择和技术参数，往往感到不知所措。此时，品牌整机（Prebuilt）便成为了省心省力的理想方案。近日，微星（MSI）推出的中端游戏主机 Codex Z2 A8NVM-485US 正在 Newegg 平台进行促销。这款主机原价 1,899 美元，现降至 1,499 美元，直降 400 美元（优惠幅度约 21%）。此外，Newegg 还提供多种配套优惠包，包括 32 英寸 4K 显示器、键鼠、无线耳机、网络摄像头甚至不间断电源（UPS），帮助用户一次性打造完整的电竞战台。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

该主机搭载的 AMD Ryzen 7 8700F 处理器基于最新的 Zen 4 架构，拥有 8 核心 16 线程，最高睿频可达 5.0 GHz。其核心创新在于集成了 AI 加速单元（NPU），这是 AMD 在桌面处理器中首次引入专用 AI 引擎，能够大幅提升本地 AI 运算任务的效率（如智能降噪、背景模糊、内容生成加速等），为未来 AI 驱动的游戏和应用做好准备。不过，该处理器并未集成显示核心，必须搭配独立显卡使用。显卡方面，MSI Codex Z2 配备了 NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Ti，拥有 8GB GDDR6 显存。该显卡支持 NVIDIA 最新的 DLSS 4.5 技术，包含多帧生成（Multi-Frame Generation）功能，能够通过 AI 深度学习算法，在几乎不牺牲画质的前提下显著提升游戏帧率；同时改进了光线追踪性能，带来更逼真的光影效果。

**性能参数与对比数据**

除了核心处理器与显卡，该主机还配备 16GB DDR5-5200 MHz 内存（双通道，频率高于常见的 DDR5-4800，可进一步提升带宽），以及 2TB M.2 NVMe Gen4 固态硬盘（SSD），能够提供极快的游戏加载速度和充裕的存储空间。电源方面采用 650W 80 Plus 金牌认证电源，能效较高，足以稳定支撑整套配置的功耗需求。散热由单塔风冷负责，机箱采用紧凑型气流优化设计，前面板为大面积金属网孔，并预装三把 120mm ARGB 风扇，侧面板为钢化玻璃，可展示内部硬件与灯效。无线连接支持 Wi-Fi 6E 和蓝牙 5.3。I/O 接口丰富：前置包含一个 USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A、一个 USB 3.2 Gen 2x2 Type-C（理论传输速度 20Gbps）以及独立的耳机和麦克风插孔；后置则提供两个 USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A、两个 USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-A、四个 USB 2.0 Type-A、音频接口（含光纤输出）、千兆以太网接口以及一个 PS/2 接口，兼容旧式键鼠。

与同价位其他整机相比，该配置在存储容量（2TB）和 Wi-Fi 支持（Wi-Fi 6E）方面具有优势，但内存仅 16GB，对于部分重度多任务或未来需求而言可能略显不足。不过，对于主流 1080p 分辨率下的现代游戏，以及许多 1440p 分辨率下的老款或优化较好的游戏，该组合可以提供充足的性能。

**技术影响与应用场景**

这款整机旨在满足中高端游戏玩家的需求，省去了玩家自行选件、兼容性检查、安装调试的繁琐过程。其搭载的 NPU 和 DLSS 4.5 技术，不仅提升了当前游戏体验（尤其是光追场景下的流畅度），也为未来更广泛地利用 AI 进行游戏内增强、画质优化甚至辅助创作（例如 AI 驱动的 Nvidia Broadcast）奠定了基础。对于正在考虑从旧平台升级的玩家，MSI Codex Z2 在促销价下提供了极具竞争力的性价比，尤其适合那些希望一步到位、开箱即玩且拥有 2TB 大容量存储的用户。若进一步关注其他硬件优惠，可查阅 Newegg 平台的 PC 硬件、SSD、硬盘、显示器、显卡、游戏椅及 CPU 等各类促销页面。

items.title: Microsoft is reportedly testing Copilot+ AI features with discrete GPUs instead of NPUs — a feature available on Windows App SDK with a Windows Insider Experimental Channel build and Developer Mode turned on
items.description: Microsoft is experimenting with Windows AI features on non-Copilot devices, finally allowing AI features to run on discrete GPUs. This move expands its user base and gives more users access to Windows 11 local AI capabilities.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Copilot PCs have been around for a couple of years since Microsoft launched them in 2024, and while the company tried to push NPU-equipped laptops towards users to take advantage of these new features, it seems that it’s planning to reverse course. According to Windows Latest, an experimental Windows App SDK available on GitHub now lets you run Language Model APIs on supported GPUs, starting with the Nvidia GeForce RTX 30-series cards with at least 6GB of VRAM. However, it also requires a Windows Insider Experimental Channel and Developer Mode switched on. Hence, you need to go through some hoops to turn on local AI inferencing on Windows, even if you don’t have an NPU-equipped device.You still won’t get all the features found in a Copilot+ PC even if you went through all the hoops to activate this feature, but it’s a sign of things to come for local AI on Windows PCs, in general. It’s still not clear why Microsoft is seemingly abandoning the Copilot+ PC advantage it has heavily marketed in recent years, though RAM pricing might play a role, but this is good news for the millions of users who were locked out of Copilot features simply because their processors don’t have a built-in NPU (neural processing unit). This also opens Windows’ AI features to desktop users, who typically don’t have processors that support NPUs.What’s interesting is that NPUs aren’t necessarily more powerful than GPUs for AI processing — it’s just that they’re more efficient, making them crucial for laptops with limited battery life. And because not all laptops come with a discrete GPU (which are mostly found on gaming and high-end laptops), it also allowed Microsoft to include AI features on more affordable Windows 11 PCs. Another reason Microsoft could be looking to expand Copilot+ features to non-NPU-powered devices is that AI didn’t actually take off the way it had hoped. A research firm in 2024 said that people didn’t purchase AI PCs because of their features — instead, they bought them because they’re what’s available if they need to upgrade.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The situation is worsening in 2026, as the AI data center-driven shortage of memory and storage chips is pushing computer prices to unprecedented highs. This has resulted in a collapse in sales for PCs and their components, with entry-level laptops expected to disappear by 2028. People not buying new NPU-equipped PCs would limit the adoption of Copilot+ PCs and the AI features they offer. By expanding the availability of AI features to non-Copilot+ PCs, Microsoft will increase its user base and help differentiate Windows 11 from the competition, especially as Windows is slowly bleeding users to macOS and Linux. items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 据报道，微软正在测试使用独立GPU而非NPU的Copilot+ AI功能——该功能可在Windows App SDK上通过Windows Insider实验版通道并开启开发者模式使用
items.descriptionZh:
自2024年微软推出Copilot PC以来，带有神经网络处理单元（NPU）的笔记本电脑已问世数年。尽管微软一直试图向用户推广配备NPU的笔记本以充分利用AI新功能，但最新迹象表明，该公司可能正在逆转这一策略。根据Windows Latest的报道，GitHub上一款实验性的Windows App SDK版本现已允许在支持的GPU上运行语言模型API，首发支持英伟达GeForce RTX 30系列显卡（需至少6GB显存）。不过，该功能需要加入Windows Insider实验通道并开启开发者模式——这意味着即使没有NPU设备，用户也可以通过一些额外步骤在Windows上启用本地AI推理。

即便成功激活该功能，用户仍无法获得Copilot+ PC的全部特性，但这预示着本地AI在Windows PC上的发展方向。目前尚不清楚微软为何似乎要放弃近年来大力推广的Copilot+ PC优势，虽然内存价格波动可能是一个因素。但这对于数百万因处理器缺少内置NPU而被Copilot功能拒之门外的用户来说无疑是个好消息。该举措也将Windows的AI特性向桌面用户开放——这些用户通常使用的处理器并不支持NPU。

值得注意的是，在AI处理能力上，NPU并不一定比GPU更强——其优势在于效率更高，这对电池续航有限的笔记本电脑至关重要。由于并非所有笔记本都配备独立GPU（多数仅见于游戏本和高端机型），微软此前通过NPU方案得以在更实惠的Windows 11 PC上集成AI功能。另一个可能的原因是，AI的普及并未达到微软的预期。2024年一家研究机构指出，用户购买AI PC并非看重其AI特性，而是因为升级需求下AI PC正好是市场上的可选项。

到2026年，情况进一步恶化——AI数据中心驱动的存储芯片短缺正将电脑价格推至前所未有的高位，导致PC及其组件的销量崩溃，预计入门级笔记本电脑将在2028年消失。用户不购买新款NPU PC将限制Copilot+ PC及AI功能的普及。通过将AI功能扩展到非Copilot+ PC，微软将扩大用户基础，并帮助Windows 11在竞争中脱颖而出——尤其是在Windows用户正缓慢流失至macOS和Linux的背景下。

这一实验性SDK的发布，意味着未来Windows PC上的本地AI功能将不再完全依赖NPU硬件。对于拥有强大GPU的台式机和高端笔记本用户而言，他们将能通过GPU加速享受本地AI推理，从而绕开硬件限制。尽管目前该功能仍处于早期实验阶段，但这标志着微软正在调整其AI战略，从硬性绑定NPU转向更开放的生态策略。

items.title: AMD taunts Apple's MacBook Neo for failing to run 75% of top PC games — Only 5 out of the 20 top PC games work on the Neo, while all run on AMD's budget offerings
items.description: AMD is reminding folks not to buy a MacBook, even if it's as good of a deal as the Neo, if you primarily want to game on it. Instead, AMD's own budget laptops can run all the modern titles you want, with a small caveat.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 12 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The MacBook Neo has been an astronomical hit for Apple, with both critics and consumers responding positively to the company's entry-level laptop. It changed the precedent for what a budget laptop could be, scaring Windows-reliant vendors, but one legacy chipmaker doesn't seem bothered. AMD has just released new marketing material that directly pits a Ryzen 200 series-equipped machine with the Neo, mocking the latter for not supporting 75% of top PC games."While 15 of the top 20 PC games do not run on Macbook Neo natively, AMD systems give you access to massive game libraries," said the company. Only 5 of those 20 titles will work on the Neo. Technically, you can use emulation software to still access more games, but AMD specifically covers that ground, too, and mentions "no workarounds required." It then points out how multiple storefronts enjoy universal compatibility with AMD devices. (Image credit: AMD)For the comparison, AMD used HP's OmniBook X Flip powered by the Ryzen 5 220. That's not a new chip; it's a refresh of the company's Hawk Point lineup (8540U), and this SKU in particular features 2 full-fat Zen 4 cores and 4x Zen4c efficiency cores, for a total of 12 threads. It's paired with a Radeon 740M iGPU that's not exactly the most powerful by modern standards. It's the same integrated graphics found in the regular Ryzen Z1 APU, so it's about 2x less performant than the Z1 Extreme.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: If you actually look at the reviews of this iGPU, it's a mixed bag. It can run older titles like GTA V at over 100 FPS at low settings, but it struggles with pretty much modern games. Hellblade 2 runs at 8 frames per second, while Alan Wake 2 hits about 11 frames on average. So yes, AMD runs 20 out of the 20 top games on PC, but a good chunk of them will not be smooth enough to be called playable on this specific chip. It's the x86 architecture and the Windows OS that are the real difference makers.Everyone knows Apple Silicon is more than powerful enough, and the company's Metal API has modern features capable of real-time rendering. But there's simply not enough demand from the community and not a big push from Apple to pitch gaming as one of the platform's selling points. You can still emulate x86 code into Arm using Parallels, Crossover, or even Game Hub, but that's not native compatibility. Therefore, AMD is correct. You may like Cyberpunk 2077 runs on MacBook Neo at over 30 FPS The $599 MacBook Neo stunned the budget laptop market M5 Max MacBook Pro paired with RTX 5090 in an eGPU dock (Image credit: AMD)The company doesn't stop there; it also highlights how the OmniBook X Flip specifically has a 512GB SSD compared to the MacBook Neo's 256GB of storage. This laptop also features a touchscreen in a 2-in-1 form factor, versus the Neo, which has just a basic screen. Lastly, the port selection is more varied with AMD as well; there are 2x USB-C, 2x USB-A, and 1x HDMI ports on the HP machine, while you only get 2x USB-C on the Neo. And, of course, the Neo doesn't come with a Windows 11 license.All that being said, the MacBook Neo is still an excellent laptop for the price. If gaming is imperative to you, Intel's Wildcat Lake laptops are also starting to penetrate this segment with similar pricing and impressive value propositions. If you're chasing that Arm efficiency, then Qualcomm's Snapdragon C-series chips also come with Windows, allowing you to at least bypass the OS difference. It's a great time to be in the market for budget laptops, but the simultaneous component crisis is still taming the full potential of this class.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Future)Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: AMD嘲讽苹果MacBook Neo无法运行75%的顶级PC游戏——20款顶级PC游戏中仅有5款能在Neo上运行，而所有游戏都能在AMD的廉价产品上运行
items.descriptionZh:
AMD近期发布了一份营销材料，直接将搭载Ryzen 200系列处理器的笔记本电脑与苹果MacBook Neo进行对比，嘲讽后者无法原生运行75%的顶级PC游戏。这一举动揭示了当前PC市场中一个核心矛盾：苹果的Arm架构芯片性能足够强大，但游戏生态的缺失使其在与x86架构设备的竞争中处于弱势。

AMD指出，在Steam平台最受欢迎的20款PC游戏中，只有5款能在MacBook Neo上原生运行。虽然用户可以通过模拟软件（如Parallels、Crossover或Game Hub）间接运行x86代码，但这并非原生兼容，且需要额外设置。AMD特意强调“无需任何变通手段”，因为其搭载Ryzen 5 220的HP OmniBook X Flip可以原生访问庞大的游戏库，并且多个游戏商店在该平台上具有通用兼容性。

从技术原理上看，MacBook Neo搭载的Apple Silicon芯片基于Arm架构，其Metal API具备现代图形渲染功能，理论上足以支持实时渲染。然而，由于游戏开发者社区对macOS平台的需求不足，加上苹果并未将游戏作为其生态的核心卖点，导致大量热门PC游戏缺乏原生支持。相反，AMD的Ryzen 200系列处理器采用x86架构，与Windows操作系统天然契合，所有主流PC游戏均可直接运行。但这里的关键在于，架构和操作系统才是决定性因素，而非单纯的芯片性能。

AMD用于对比的Ryzen 5 220并非全新芯片，而是Hawk Point系列（8540U）的升级版。它配备了2个全性能Zen 4核心和4个能效Zen 4c核心，共12线程，集成Radeon 740M核显（与标准Ryzen Z1 APU相同，性能约为Z1 Extreme的一半）。实际游戏性能表现参差不齐：在低画质下，GTA V可超过100帧，但面对现代3A大作则力不从心——《地狱之刃2》平均仅8帧，《心灵杀手2》约11帧。因此，虽然AMD声称可以运行全部20款顶级PC游戏，但其中不少游戏在该芯片上几乎无法流畅游玩。

除了游戏兼容性，AMD还突出了其他硬件差异：HP OmniBook X Flip标配512GB SSD（MacBook Neo仅256GB）、配备触摸屏和2合1变形设计、提供2个USB-C、2个USB-A和1个HDMI接口（Neo只有2个USB-C），且预装Windows 11授权。这些对比意在说明，在同等价位下，AMD设备提供了更丰富的功能和扩展性。

尽管如此，MacBook Neo在低价笔记本电脑市场中依然是一款出色的产品。如果游戏是用户的刚需，除了AMD方案，Intel的Wildcat Lake系列笔记本也已开始以类似价格渗透这一领域，并带来不错的性价比。而追求Arm架构能效的用户，则可以考虑搭载高通Snapdragon C系列芯片的Windows笔记本，至少能绕开操作系统差异。当前低价笔记本市场竞争激烈，但全球元件危机仍在限制该品类的全面潜力。对于消费者而言，这是多元选择的好时代，但必须根据自身需求权衡游戏生态、存储扩展、端口配置等关键因素。

items.title: AI cryptomining network's 320,000 RTX 3090-class GPUs allegedly burn 112 megawatts of power on ‘zero useful AI computation’ — GPU rental costs jump 38%, but Pearl’s cards are doing random matrix math, study claims
items.description: A preprint claims Pearl’s AI mining network consumes 320,000 GPU-equivalents and 112 MW while producing no verified useful AI computation.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A new research preprint claims that Pearl, a Layer-1 blockchain marketed as turning cryptocurrency mining into useful artificial intelligence (AI) computation, is doing nothing of the sort, despite having recently triggered a GPU mining rush. The study estimates Pearl's network runs at roughly 24 exahashes per second (EH/s) — the equivalent of about 320,000 RTX 3090-class GPUs drawing an estimated 112 megawatts (MW) — all while producing "zero useful AI computation." Researchers saw a roughly 38% jump in budget GPU rental prices on the marketplace vast.ai go to Pearl mining, with utilization climbing from 57% to 94% after the mining software went public in May.Instead of performing real inference or training, the paper, titled The Usefulness Gap in Proof-of-Useful-Work, says the network grinds through random matrix multiplications that merely take the shape of AI math. The self-billed “first empirical measurement of a deployed Proof-of-Useful-Work (PoUW) system” study argues that Pearl’s mining protocol verifies that miners performed matrix multiplication correctly, but does not verify whether that work came from real AI training or inference workloads.Pearl swaps Bitcoin's SHA-256 hashing for a scheme it calls cuPOW, which asks miners to compute noised integer matrix multiplications and prove they did so correctly. That operation is the same arithmetic that underpins neural network inference and training, which is the foundation of Pearl's pitch that mining and AI compute can be one and the same job. The problem, according to the study, is that the protocol's verification step only confirms that the multiplication was performed correctly. It never checks whether the input matrices came from a real model, a paying customer, or any AI workload at all.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: To demonstrate that gap, the researcher, Abhinaba Basu, built a miner that feeds the network uniformly random matrices with no inference attached, then submitted the output to a mining pool. The paper reports 44 pool-accepted shares on Nvidia and AMD hardware, with the same miner also benchmarked on a CPU and Apple Silicon, plus an on-chain payout earned by running the standard mining software unmodified. If random numbers collect rewards as readily as genuine AI work, the argument runs, then the network cannot tell the two apart, and miners have every incentive to skip the AI part entirely.Basu also analyzed 8,012 workers in a single pool, representing about 21% of Pearl's hashrate, and found that all of them ran hardware capable of AI inference. Yet, the dominant mining binary contained no identifiable code for any machine-learning framework. That binary analysis relies on string inspection, which the paper notes can be defeated by stripped or obfuscated code, so the finding is offered as strong evidence rather than outright proof. Runtime profiling pointed the same way, with the miners showing heavy compute use and light memory-bandwidth use, a signature consistent with pure matrix math and inconsistent with the memory-hungry behavior of transformer inference. You may like New AI-compute cryptocurrency Pearl sparks a GPU mining rush but profitability is already sliding Iran conflict spurs Bitcoin mining operators to pivot to AI infrastructure Tests show $30,000 AI GPUs are terrible password crackers — RTX 5090 gaming GPU outperforms Nvidia H200 and AMD MI300X For GPU buyers, the resource angle is the part that stings. The study attributes a roughly 38% jump in budget GPU rental prices on the marketplace vast.ai to Pearl mining, with utilization climbing from 57% to 94% after the mining software went public in May. Using a difference-in-differences comparison against pricier datacenter cards, Basu estimates around $600,000 per year in additional rental costs borne by independent researchers who compete for the same cheap hardware. However, he cautions that the figure depends on assumptions about how stable prices were beforehand. At PRL's recent price near $0.76, the paper calculates that mining is marginally profitable on budget cards such as the RTX 3060 Ti and roughly breakeven on an RTX 3090.The work also chips away at the assumption that Pearl mining is an Nvidia-only affair. Basu reports the first Pearl shares ever mined on non-Nvidia hardware, driving an AMD Instinct MI300X at 10.6 million tiles per second, faster than the closed-source Nvidia miner managed on an RTX 3090, and benchmarking the same workload on a server CPU and on an Apple M2 through Metal compute shaders. Because the computation is commodity integer arithmetic, the paper argues there is no vendor lock-in and no technical reason for the work to remain on any one company's silicon.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsPearl may have a ready response, but the study takes it on directly. Together AI, which announced an exclusive partnership in May, framed the deal as letting "every GPU cycle powering AI training and inference" also mint the PRL token, and it now offers a discounted Gemma-4-31B-it-pearl inference endpoint subsidized by mining proceeds. Basu counters that this is financial arbitrage rather than useful mining, because Together AI's own GPUs perform that inference separately from the mining network, with PRL revenue used to trim the endpoint's price. The 8,012 mining workers he measured, he says, produced none of that inference themselves.The study’s conclusion is not that Proof-of-Useful-Work is impossible, but that Pearl’s current design leaves a major enforcement gap. The protocol enables useful work in theory, but it does not require it in practice. That leaves Pearl in an uncomfortable middle ground — it performs real computation, but according to the paper, the network currently has no way to prove that the computation is useful AI work rather than cryptomining with AI-shaped math. items.author: Etiido Uko
items.titleZh: AI加密货币挖矿网络32万块RTX 3090级GPU据称消耗112兆瓦电力，用于“零有效AI计算”——GPU租赁成本上涨38%，但研究声称Pearl的显卡在进行随机矩阵运算
items.descriptionZh:
一项最新研究预印本声称，名为Pearl的Layer-1区块链——其营销口号是将加密货币挖矿转化为有用的人工智能（AI）计算——实际上并未实现这一目标，尽管它近期引发了GPU挖矿热潮。这项研究估算，Pearl网络的算力约为每秒24 exahash（EH/s），相当于约32万张RTX 3090级别的GPU，消耗约112兆瓦电力，但“产生的有用AI计算为零”。研究人员观察到，自5月挖矿软件公开后，算力市场vast.ai上的廉价GPU租赁价格上涨了约38%，利用率从57%攀升至94%。

这篇题为《有用工作量证明的实用性鸿沟》的论文指出，Pearl网络并非执行真正的推理或训练，而是在进行随机矩阵乘法，这种运算仅仅具有AI数学的形式。作为“首个对已部署的有用工作量证明（PoUW）系统进行的实证测量”研究，该论文认为，Pearl的挖矿协议验证了矿工正确执行了矩阵乘法，但并未验证这些工作是否来自真实的AI训练或推理负载。Pearl用其名为cuPOW的方案取代了比特币的SHA-256哈希算法，该方案要求矿工计算带噪声的整数矩阵乘法，并证明其计算正确。这种运算正是神经网络推理和训练的底层算术——这也是Pearl声称挖矿与AI计算可以合二为一的基础。然而，问题在于协议验证步骤只确认了乘法是否被正确执行，它从不检查输入矩阵是否来自真实模型、付费客户或任何AI负载。

为证明这一缺口，研究人员Abhinaba Basu构建了一个矿工程序，向网络输入均匀随机矩阵（不附带任何推理），然后将输出提交至矿池。论文报告称，该矿工在NVIDIA和AMD硬件上获得了44个矿池接受的份额，并在CPU和Apple Silicon上进行了相同负载的基准测试，同时还通过运行未经修改的标准挖矿软件获得了链上奖励。论文的核心论点是：如果随机数也能像真实的AI工作一样轻易获得奖励，那么网络就无法区分二者，矿工便有充分的动机完全跳过AI部分。

Basu还分析了单个矿池中的8,012个工人（代表Pearl约21%的算力），发现所有工人都具备运行AI推理的硬件能力，但主流挖矿二进制文件中不含任何可识别的机器学习框架代码。该二进制分析依赖于字符串检查，论文指出这可能会被剥离或混淆的代码绕过，因此该发现被视为强有力的证据而非确凿证明。运行时分析也指向同一结论：矿工程序表现出高计算负载和低内存带宽使用，这与纯矩阵运算的特征一致，而与传统Transformer推理的内存密集型行为不符。

对GPU购买者而言，资源层面的影响最为刺痛。研究将vast.ai上廉价GPU租赁价格约38%的上涨归因于Pearl挖矿，自5月挖矿软件公开后，利用率从57%升至94%。通过将更昂贵的数据中心显卡进行双重差分对比，Basu估计独立研究人员每年额外承担约60万美元的租赁成本——不过他也谨慎指出，该数字依赖于对先前价格稳定性的假设。以PRL近期约0.76美元的价格计算，论文估算在RTX 3060 Ti等廉价显卡上挖矿勉强盈利，在RTX 3090上则大致持平。

该工作还打破了Pearl挖矿仅限NVIDIA硬件的假设。Basu报告了首个在非NVIDIA硬件上挖出的Pearl份额：在AMD Instinct MI300X上达到每秒1060万tiles，超过了闭源NVIDIA矿工在RTX 3090上的表现；同时在同一负载下对服务器CPU和Apple M2（通过Metal计算着色器）进行了基准测试。由于计算本身是通用整数算术，论文认为不存在供应商锁定，也没有技术理由将工作局限于某一家公司的芯片。

对于Pearl可能的回应，研究直接进行了反驳。Together AI在5月宣布与Pearl建立独家合作，声称该合作伙伴关系让“每一条驱动AI训练和推理的GPU周期”都能同时铸造PRL代币，并推出了一种由挖矿收益补贴的打折Gemma-4-31B-it-pearl推理端点。Basu反驳称，这本质上是金融套利而非有用挖矿，因为Together AI自己的GPU独立于挖矿网络执行推理，只是用PRL收入来降低端点价格。他测量的那8,012个挖矿工人，没有产生任何推理工作。

研究的结论并非“有用工作量证明”不可能实现，而是Pearl当前的设计存在重大执行缺口。该协议在理论上支持有用工作，但在实践中并未强制要求。这使得Pearl陷入了一个尴尬的中间地带——它执行了真实计算，但根据论文，该网络目前无法证明这些计算是有用的AI工作，而非只是披着AI数学外衣的加密货币挖矿。

history.title: Intel's upcoming 'Raptor Lake Next' will reportedly top out at 20 cores and retain Core 200 branding — Lineup may include a special 10-core SKU with 24MB of L3 cache
history.description: Intel's Raptor Lake family might be coming back for a third time and sit alongside Nova Lake on shelves as the budget-oriented offering from the company.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 7 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Yesterday, we broke news of a third Raptor Lake refresh in the works from Intel, dubbed "Raptor Lake Next." Following that, new leaks have emerged from reliable tipster Jaykihn confirming the codename and detailing the upcoming family. Raptor Lake Next is slated for early 2027 and will land on the LGA 1700 socket as reported previously, but some outlets say it will also retain the existing Core 200 branding.- Raptor Lake silicon, not Bartlett Lake- Early next year- Desktop and mobile (125W, 65W; HX)- Coexists with 14th generation availability- No fresh features from RPL-R. For comparison, RPL-R had many from RPL, such as WiFi 7, Fast Throttle, APO, etc.Specs soon.June 13, 2026According to the rumors, Raptor Lake Next doesn't use Bartlett Lake silicon, which is Intel's special lineup for edge and embedded devices with only P-cores. That means a 12 P-core SKU has been ruled out for now, and that Raptor Lake Next will stick to the usual hybrid config. It's expected to scale across mobile and desktop with up to 125W parts and HX models as well, but it won't bring any new notable features.Essentially, we're looking at rebranded 14th Gen CPUs that are likely being re-released because of their high yields. Intel spent a considerable amount of time and effort fixing the stability issues with 13th and 14th Gen families, so it makes sense the company wants to maximize its returns here. The Core-i9 14900K is still the most performant gaming CPU Intel has ever released, so calling this leftover silicon doesn't feel right either.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Jaykihn also provided further specs for the Raptor Lake Next, claiming it will only spread across Core 3, Core 5, and Core 7; no Core 9 SKUs in sight at the moment. At the top sits a Core 7 part with 8 P-cores and 12 E-cores, totaling out to 20 cores rated at 65W, same as the Core i7-14700. Then there's a 16-core 125W processor, which is identical to the Core i7-13700K, but it's counted as "Core 5" for Raptor Lake Next.Raptor Lake Next Desktop iGPU Enabled SKUsC7 8+12 65WC5 8+8 125WC5 6+4 65W 24MB L3C3 4+0 65WThe i5 6+4 65W 24MB L3 is a new configuration (Default 20MB L3 for 6+4).The closest alternatives are the 13490f/14490f, which do not have integrated graphics enabled. https://t.co/Q5nzFtSMTvJune 14, 2026There's a special 10-core 65W part with 24MB of L3 cache because the previous 10-core silicon only had 20MB. It looks like the cache from disabled core clusters will still be accessible in certain Raptor Lake Next SKUs, and this might become a trend going forward. Lastly, we have a Core 3 CPU with 4 P-cores and no E-cores, running at 65W as well. Since all these chips are based on Raptor Lake silicon, they use Raptor Cove P-cores and Gracemont E-cores. You may like Intel reportedly preparing surprise return to DDR4 systems with 'Raptor Lake Next' Intel will reportedly upgrade to an 8-core config for its Wildcat Lake refresh next year Intel reportedly no longer working on 6-core Nova Lake mobile SKU, alleges new rumor Keep in mind that RTL and RTL-R both came with Intel HD 700 series integrated graphics; the new Arc Xe architecture debuted with Arrow Lake on desktop. Nonetheless, Raptor Lake Next will work with DDR4 memory because it uses the LGA 1700 platform. That could help alleviate at least some of the burden of building a PC today as DDR5 RAM doesn't seem to be getting cheaper anytime soon.Raptor Lake Next is apparently going into production in January 2027 so it could be available in Q1 2027, coexisting with Nova Lake as perhaps the value-focused option from the Blue Team. All rumors point toward Nova Lake being delayed to next year, but officially we still expect an announcement later this year. Regardless, the two families sure look like they'll overlap, which is why the Core 200 vs Core 400 distinction would be important for consumers.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 英特尔即将推出的“Raptor Lake Next”据报道将最高配备20核心，并沿用Core 200系列品牌——产品线可能包含一款特殊的10核SKU，配备24MB L3缓存
history.descriptionZh:
Intel计划在2027年初推出“Raptor Lake Next”，这是其第13/14代酷睿（Raptor Lake）架构的第三次刷新版本，将沿用LGA 1700接口，并继续使用现有的“Core 200”系列品牌命名。此消息由可靠爆料人Jaykihn证实，继昨日我们报道了Intel正在开发第三版Raptor Lake的消息后进一步披露了更多细节。

**背景与上下文**：Raptor Lake Next并非基于此前为边缘和嵌入式设备设计、仅配备P核的Bartlett Lake芯片，而是沿用标准的大小核混合架构（Raptor Cove P核 + Gracemont E核）。这意味着此前传闻的12个P核顶级型号已被排除。该系列将覆盖台式机和移动平台，包括最高125W功耗的SKU和HX系列，但不会引入任何新特性——相比之下，上一代RPL-R（Raptor Lake Refresh）曾带来WiFi 7、Fast Throttle、APO等新功能。本质上，Raptor Lake Next是对第14代酷睿的重命名，很可能是因为Intel在花费大量时间精力修复13/14代稳定性问题后，希望利用高良率芯片最大化回报。Core i9-14900K仍然是Intel史上最强游戏处理器，因此称之为“剩余芯片”并不恰当。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：技术上没有创新，它是基于成熟Raptor Lake硅片的再发布。所有芯片均使用Raptor Cove P核和Gracemont E核，集成Intel HD 700系列显卡（而非Arrow Lake上的全新Arc Xe架构）。值得注意的是，由于采用LGA 1700平台，Raptor Lake Next将支持DDR4内存，在当前DDR5价格居高不下的背景下，这有助于降低整机搭建成本。另外，部分SKU的L3缓存有所增加：例如10核型号（6P+4E，65W）配备了24MB L3缓存，而此前同配置仅有20MB；这表明某些型号中，从禁用核心簇中释放的缓存仍可被利用，可能成为未来趋势。

**性能参数与对比数据**：Jaykihn提供了详细规格，Raptor Lake Next仅覆盖Core 3、Core 5和Core 7系列，目前无Core 9型号。顶级型号为Core 7：8个P核+12个E核，共20核，65W功耗，与Core i7-14700相同。次旗舰为Core 5：8个P核+8个E核，共16核，125W功耗，与Core i7-13700K相同。此外还有一款特殊的Core 5：6个P核+4个E核，65W，24MB L3缓存（此前类似配置如13490F/14490F的L3为20MB，且无集成显卡）。最低端为Core 3：4个P核，无E核，65W。所有型号均集成显卡。

**技术影响与应用场景**：Raptor Lake Next计划于2027年1月进入量产，预计2027年第一季度上市。它将与Intel下一代架构Nova Lake共存，作为面向预算市场的“蓝色阵营价值选择”。考虑到Nova Lake可能延期至2027年之后，两个家族将出现明显重叠，此时Core 200与Core 400的命名区分对消费者至关重要。对于计划升级的PC用户，Raptor Lake Next提供了一个成熟、稳定且兼容DDR4的性价比平台，尤其适合游戏和主流办公场景，但要注意它不包含任何新架构特性（如Arrow Lake的改进能效或新GPU）。整体而言，这是Intel在过渡期的务实战略，通过延长成熟架构生命周期来平衡研发投入和市场需求。

history.title: Grab this MSI Codex Z2 16-thread gaming Ryzen PC with a 2TB SSD at a $400 discount — system packs a Ryzen 8700F, 16GB DDR5, and RTX 5060 Ti 8GB for $1,499
history.description: MSI's prebuilt gaming desktop pairs a Zen 4 processor with Nvidia's latest RTX 5060 Ti graphics card and comes housed in an airflow-focused chassis with ARGB lighting.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Building a gaming PC can be an overwhelming task, especially if it's your first one, as the sheer number of choices and technical specifications can make the process feel intimidating. That's where prebuilts come to the rescue, and today we have a solid mid-range offering from MSI that is currently on sale at Newegg. Originally priced at $1,899, the MSI Codex Z2 A8NVM-485US is down to $1,499, offering savings of $400. Newegg is also offering special bundles with this prebuilt PC, including a 32-inch 4K monitor, keyboard, mouse, wireless headset, webcam, and even a UPS, helping you complete your next battlestation. Check out this deal on Newegg The MSI Codex Z2 comes with a compact airflow-focused chassis with a large mesh panel at the front with triple 120mm ARGB fans as well as a glass side panel to showcase the internals. The PC is powered by an AMD Ryzen 7 8700F, a powerful Zen 4 processor featuring 8 cores, 16 threads, and an AI-accelerating NPU. It is capable of hitting up to 5 GHz boost clocks, however, it does not come with an integrated graphics solution. For graphics you get the Nvidia RTX 5060 Ti 8GB offering all the latest tech including DLSS 4.5 with multi-frame generation, improved ray tracing performance, and more. Save 21% ($400)MSI Codex Z2 A8NVM-485US : was $1,899 now $1,499 at NeweggThe MSI Condex Z2 powered by the Ryzen 7 8700F and RTX 5060 Ti is currently selling at $1,499 saving you $400 and the hassle of choosing the right parts and assembling them. View Deal The rest of the features include 16GB of DDR5-5200 RAM, a 2TB M.2 NVMe Gen4 SSD to store all your games, and a 650W 80 Plus Gold power supply which should offer enough juice to power all the hardware. The CPU is cooled using a single-tower air cooler and in terms of wireless connectivity the system comes with built-in Wi-Fi 6E and Bluetooth 5.3. I/O options include a USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A, a USB 3.2 Gen 2x2 Type-C, along with dedicated headphone and mic ports at the front. At the rear you get two USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A, two USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-A, four USB 2.0 Type-A, audio ports including optical, gigabit Ethernet, and even a PS/2 port. If you have been considering a gaming PC upgrade from an older generation, the MSI Codex Z2 is worth a look at its discounted price of $1,499. The combination of a Ryzen 7 8700F, RTX 5060 Ti, and 16GB of DDR5 memory should provide plenty of horsepower for modern games at 1080p and some older titles in 1440p resolution. history.author: Kunal Khullar
history.titleZh: 抓住这个微星Codex Z2 16线程游戏Ryzen电脑，配备2TB固态硬盘，直降400美元——系统搭载Ryzen 8700F、16GB DDR5和RTX 5060 Ti 8GB，售价1,499美元
history.descriptionZh:
MSI Codex Z2 A8NVM-485US 游戏主机降价促销：搭载 Ryzen 7 8700F 与 RTX 5060 Ti，售价降至 1,499 美元

对于初次组装游戏 PC 的玩家而言，面对琳琅满目的硬件选择和技术参数，往往感到不知所措。此时，品牌整机（Prebuilt）便成为了省心省力的理想方案。近日，微星（MSI）推出的中端游戏主机 Codex Z2 A8NVM-485US 正在 Newegg 平台进行促销。这款主机原价 1,899 美元，现降至 1,499 美元，直降 400 美元（优惠幅度约 21%）。此外，Newegg 还提供多种配套优惠包，包括 32 英寸 4K 显示器、键鼠、无线耳机、网络摄像头甚至不间断电源（UPS），帮助用户一次性打造完整的电竞战台。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

该主机搭载的 AMD Ryzen 7 8700F 处理器基于最新的 Zen 4 架构，拥有 8 核心 16 线程，最高睿频可达 5.0 GHz。其核心创新在于集成了 AI 加速单元（NPU），这是 AMD 在桌面处理器中首次引入专用 AI 引擎，能够大幅提升本地 AI 运算任务的效率（如智能降噪、背景模糊、内容生成加速等），为未来 AI 驱动的游戏和应用做好准备。不过，该处理器并未集成显示核心，必须搭配独立显卡使用。显卡方面，MSI Codex Z2 配备了 NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Ti，拥有 8GB GDDR6 显存。该显卡支持 NVIDIA 最新的 DLSS 4.5 技术，包含多帧生成（Multi-Frame Generation）功能，能够通过 AI 深度学习算法，在几乎不牺牲画质的前提下显著提升游戏帧率；同时改进了光线追踪性能，带来更逼真的光影效果。

**性能参数与对比数据**

除了核心处理器与显卡，该主机还配备 16GB DDR5-5200 MHz 内存（双通道，频率高于常见的 DDR5-4800，可进一步提升带宽），以及 2TB M.2 NVMe Gen4 固态硬盘（SSD），能够提供极快的游戏加载速度和充裕的存储空间。电源方面采用 650W 80 Plus 金牌认证电源，能效较高，足以稳定支撑整套配置的功耗需求。散热由单塔风冷负责，机箱采用紧凑型气流优化设计，前面板为大面积金属网孔，并预装三把 120mm ARGB 风扇，侧面板为钢化玻璃，可展示内部硬件与灯效。无线连接支持 Wi-Fi 6E 和蓝牙 5.3。I/O 接口丰富：前置包含一个 USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A、一个 USB 3.2 Gen 2x2 Type-C（理论传输速度 20Gbps）以及独立的耳机和麦克风插孔；后置则提供两个 USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A、两个 USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-A、四个 USB 2.0 Type-A、音频接口（含光纤输出）、千兆以太网接口以及一个 PS/2 接口，兼容旧式键鼠。

与同价位其他整机相比，该配置在存储容量（2TB）和 Wi-Fi 支持（Wi-Fi 6E）方面具有优势，但内存仅 16GB，对于部分重度多任务或未来需求而言可能略显不足。不过，对于主流 1080p 分辨率下的现代游戏，以及许多 1440p 分辨率下的老款或优化较好的游戏，该组合可以提供充足的性能。

**技术影响与应用场景**

这款整机旨在满足中高端游戏玩家的需求，省去了玩家自行选件、兼容性检查、安装调试的繁琐过程。其搭载的 NPU 和 DLSS 4.5 技术，不仅提升了当前游戏体验（尤其是光追场景下的流畅度），也为未来更广泛地利用 AI 进行游戏内增强、画质优化甚至辅助创作（例如 AI 驱动的 Nvidia Broadcast）奠定了基础。对于正在考虑从旧平台升级的玩家，MSI Codex Z2 在促销价下提供了极具竞争力的性价比，尤其适合那些希望一步到位、开箱即玩且拥有 2TB 大容量存储的用户。若进一步关注其他硬件优惠，可查阅 Newegg 平台的 PC 硬件、SSD、硬盘、显示器、显卡、游戏椅及 CPU 等各类促销页面。

history.title: Microsoft is reportedly testing Copilot+ AI features with discrete GPUs instead of NPUs — a feature available on Windows App SDK with a Windows Insider Experimental Channel build and Developer Mode turned on
history.description: Microsoft is experimenting with Windows AI features on non-Copilot devices, finally allowing AI features to run on discrete GPUs. This move expands its user base and gives more users access to Windows 11 local AI capabilities.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Copilot PCs have been around for a couple of years since Microsoft launched them in 2024, and while the company tried to push NPU-equipped laptops towards users to take advantage of these new features, it seems that it’s planning to reverse course. According to Windows Latest, an experimental Windows App SDK available on GitHub now lets you run Language Model APIs on supported GPUs, starting with the Nvidia GeForce RTX 30-series cards with at least 6GB of VRAM. However, it also requires a Windows Insider Experimental Channel and Developer Mode switched on. Hence, you need to go through some hoops to turn on local AI inferencing on Windows, even if you don’t have an NPU-equipped device.You still won’t get all the features found in a Copilot+ PC even if you went through all the hoops to activate this feature, but it’s a sign of things to come for local AI on Windows PCs, in general. It’s still not clear why Microsoft is seemingly abandoning the Copilot+ PC advantage it has heavily marketed in recent years, though RAM pricing might play a role, but this is good news for the millions of users who were locked out of Copilot features simply because their processors don’t have a built-in NPU (neural processing unit). This also opens Windows’ AI features to desktop users, who typically don’t have processors that support NPUs.What’s interesting is that NPUs aren’t necessarily more powerful than GPUs for AI processing — it’s just that they’re more efficient, making them crucial for laptops with limited battery life. And because not all laptops come with a discrete GPU (which are mostly found on gaming and high-end laptops), it also allowed Microsoft to include AI features on more affordable Windows 11 PCs. Another reason Microsoft could be looking to expand Copilot+ features to non-NPU-powered devices is that AI didn’t actually take off the way it had hoped. A research firm in 2024 said that people didn’t purchase AI PCs because of their features — instead, they bought them because they’re what’s available if they need to upgrade.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The situation is worsening in 2026, as the AI data center-driven shortage of memory and storage chips is pushing computer prices to unprecedented highs. This has resulted in a collapse in sales for PCs and their components, with entry-level laptops expected to disappear by 2028. People not buying new NPU-equipped PCs would limit the adoption of Copilot+ PCs and the AI features they offer. By expanding the availability of AI features to non-Copilot+ PCs, Microsoft will increase its user base and help differentiate Windows 11 from the competition, especially as Windows is slowly bleeding users to macOS and Linux. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 据报道，微软正在测试使用独立GPU而非NPU的Copilot+ AI功能——该功能可在Windows App SDK上通过Windows Insider实验版通道并开启开发者模式使用
history.descriptionZh:
自2024年微软推出Copilot PC以来，带有神经网络处理单元（NPU）的笔记本电脑已问世数年。尽管微软一直试图向用户推广配备NPU的笔记本以充分利用AI新功能，但最新迹象表明，该公司可能正在逆转这一策略。根据Windows Latest的报道，GitHub上一款实验性的Windows App SDK版本现已允许在支持的GPU上运行语言模型API，首发支持英伟达GeForce RTX 30系列显卡（需至少6GB显存）。不过，该功能需要加入Windows Insider实验通道并开启开发者模式——这意味着即使没有NPU设备，用户也可以通过一些额外步骤在Windows上启用本地AI推理。

即便成功激活该功能，用户仍无法获得Copilot+ PC的全部特性，但这预示着本地AI在Windows PC上的发展方向。目前尚不清楚微软为何似乎要放弃近年来大力推广的Copilot+ PC优势，虽然内存价格波动可能是一个因素。但这对于数百万因处理器缺少内置NPU而被Copilot功能拒之门外的用户来说无疑是个好消息。该举措也将Windows的AI特性向桌面用户开放——这些用户通常使用的处理器并不支持NPU。

值得注意的是，在AI处理能力上，NPU并不一定比GPU更强——其优势在于效率更高，这对电池续航有限的笔记本电脑至关重要。由于并非所有笔记本都配备独立GPU（多数仅见于游戏本和高端机型），微软此前通过NPU方案得以在更实惠的Windows 11 PC上集成AI功能。另一个可能的原因是，AI的普及并未达到微软的预期。2024年一家研究机构指出，用户购买AI PC并非看重其AI特性，而是因为升级需求下AI PC正好是市场上的可选项。

到2026年，情况进一步恶化——AI数据中心驱动的存储芯片短缺正将电脑价格推至前所未有的高位，导致PC及其组件的销量崩溃，预计入门级笔记本电脑将在2028年消失。用户不购买新款NPU PC将限制Copilot+ PC及AI功能的普及。通过将AI功能扩展到非Copilot+ PC，微软将扩大用户基础，并帮助Windows 11在竞争中脱颖而出——尤其是在Windows用户正缓慢流失至macOS和Linux的背景下。

这一实验性SDK的发布，意味着未来Windows PC上的本地AI功能将不再完全依赖NPU硬件。对于拥有强大GPU的台式机和高端笔记本用户而言，他们将能通过GPU加速享受本地AI推理，从而绕开硬件限制。尽管目前该功能仍处于早期实验阶段，但这标志着微软正在调整其AI战略，从硬性绑定NPU转向更开放的生态策略。

history.title: AMD taunts Apple's MacBook Neo for failing to run 75% of top PC games — Only 5 out of the 20 top PC games work on the Neo, while all run on AMD's budget offerings
history.description: AMD is reminding folks not to buy a MacBook, even if it's as good of a deal as the Neo, if you primarily want to game on it. Instead, AMD's own budget laptops can run all the modern titles you want, with a small caveat.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 12 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The MacBook Neo has been an astronomical hit for Apple, with both critics and consumers responding positively to the company's entry-level laptop. It changed the precedent for what a budget laptop could be, scaring Windows-reliant vendors, but one legacy chipmaker doesn't seem bothered. AMD has just released new marketing material that directly pits a Ryzen 200 series-equipped machine with the Neo, mocking the latter for not supporting 75% of top PC games."While 15 of the top 20 PC games do not run on Macbook Neo natively, AMD systems give you access to massive game libraries," said the company. Only 5 of those 20 titles will work on the Neo. Technically, you can use emulation software to still access more games, but AMD specifically covers that ground, too, and mentions "no workarounds required." It then points out how multiple storefronts enjoy universal compatibility with AMD devices. (Image credit: AMD)For the comparison, AMD used HP's OmniBook X Flip powered by the Ryzen 5 220. That's not a new chip; it's a refresh of the company's Hawk Point lineup (8540U), and this SKU in particular features 2 full-fat Zen 4 cores and 4x Zen4c efficiency cores, for a total of 12 threads. It's paired with a Radeon 740M iGPU that's not exactly the most powerful by modern standards. It's the same integrated graphics found in the regular Ryzen Z1 APU, so it's about 2x less performant than the Z1 Extreme.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: If you actually look at the reviews of this iGPU, it's a mixed bag. It can run older titles like GTA V at over 100 FPS at low settings, but it struggles with pretty much modern games. Hellblade 2 runs at 8 frames per second, while Alan Wake 2 hits about 11 frames on average. So yes, AMD runs 20 out of the 20 top games on PC, but a good chunk of them will not be smooth enough to be called playable on this specific chip. It's the x86 architecture and the Windows OS that are the real difference makers.Everyone knows Apple Silicon is more than powerful enough, and the company's Metal API has modern features capable of real-time rendering. But there's simply not enough demand from the community and not a big push from Apple to pitch gaming as one of the platform's selling points. You can still emulate x86 code into Arm using Parallels, Crossover, or even Game Hub, but that's not native compatibility. Therefore, AMD is correct. You may like Cyberpunk 2077 runs on MacBook Neo at over 30 FPS The $599 MacBook Neo stunned the budget laptop market M5 Max MacBook Pro paired with RTX 5090 in an eGPU dock (Image credit: AMD)The company doesn't stop there; it also highlights how the OmniBook X Flip specifically has a 512GB SSD compared to the MacBook Neo's 256GB of storage. This laptop also features a touchscreen in a 2-in-1 form factor, versus the Neo, which has just a basic screen. Lastly, the port selection is more varied with AMD as well; there are 2x USB-C, 2x USB-A, and 1x HDMI ports on the HP machine, while you only get 2x USB-C on the Neo. And, of course, the Neo doesn't come with a Windows 11 license.All that being said, the MacBook Neo is still an excellent laptop for the price. If gaming is imperative to you, Intel's Wildcat Lake laptops are also starting to penetrate this segment with similar pricing and impressive value propositions. If you're chasing that Arm efficiency, then Qualcomm's Snapdragon C-series chips also come with Windows, allowing you to at least bypass the OS difference. It's a great time to be in the market for budget laptops, but the simultaneous component crisis is still taming the full potential of this class.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Future)Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: AMD嘲讽苹果MacBook Neo无法运行75%的顶级PC游戏——20款顶级PC游戏中仅有5款能在Neo上运行，而所有游戏都能在AMD的廉价产品上运行
history.descriptionZh:
AMD近期发布了一份营销材料，直接将搭载Ryzen 200系列处理器的笔记本电脑与苹果MacBook Neo进行对比，嘲讽后者无法原生运行75%的顶级PC游戏。这一举动揭示了当前PC市场中一个核心矛盾：苹果的Arm架构芯片性能足够强大，但游戏生态的缺失使其在与x86架构设备的竞争中处于弱势。

AMD指出，在Steam平台最受欢迎的20款PC游戏中，只有5款能在MacBook Neo上原生运行。虽然用户可以通过模拟软件（如Parallels、Crossover或Game Hub）间接运行x86代码，但这并非原生兼容，且需要额外设置。AMD特意强调“无需任何变通手段”，因为其搭载Ryzen 5 220的HP OmniBook X Flip可以原生访问庞大的游戏库，并且多个游戏商店在该平台上具有通用兼容性。

从技术原理上看，MacBook Neo搭载的Apple Silicon芯片基于Arm架构，其Metal API具备现代图形渲染功能，理论上足以支持实时渲染。然而，由于游戏开发者社区对macOS平台的需求不足，加上苹果并未将游戏作为其生态的核心卖点，导致大量热门PC游戏缺乏原生支持。相反，AMD的Ryzen 200系列处理器采用x86架构，与Windows操作系统天然契合，所有主流PC游戏均可直接运行。但这里的关键在于，架构和操作系统才是决定性因素，而非单纯的芯片性能。

AMD用于对比的Ryzen 5 220并非全新芯片，而是Hawk Point系列（8540U）的升级版。它配备了2个全性能Zen 4核心和4个能效Zen 4c核心，共12线程，集成Radeon 740M核显（与标准Ryzen Z1 APU相同，性能约为Z1 Extreme的一半）。实际游戏性能表现参差不齐：在低画质下，GTA V可超过100帧，但面对现代3A大作则力不从心——《地狱之刃2》平均仅8帧，《心灵杀手2》约11帧。因此，虽然AMD声称可以运行全部20款顶级PC游戏，但其中不少游戏在该芯片上几乎无法流畅游玩。

除了游戏兼容性，AMD还突出了其他硬件差异：HP OmniBook X Flip标配512GB SSD（MacBook Neo仅256GB）、配备触摸屏和2合1变形设计、提供2个USB-C、2个USB-A和1个HDMI接口（Neo只有2个USB-C），且预装Windows 11授权。这些对比意在说明，在同等价位下，AMD设备提供了更丰富的功能和扩展性。

尽管如此，MacBook Neo在低价笔记本电脑市场中依然是一款出色的产品。如果游戏是用户的刚需，除了AMD方案，Intel的Wildcat Lake系列笔记本也已开始以类似价格渗透这一领域，并带来不错的性价比。而追求Arm架构能效的用户，则可以考虑搭载高通Snapdragon C系列芯片的Windows笔记本，至少能绕开操作系统差异。当前低价笔记本市场竞争激烈，但全球元件危机仍在限制该品类的全面潜力。对于消费者而言，这是多元选择的好时代，但必须根据自身需求权衡游戏生态、存储扩展、端口配置等关键因素。

history.title: AI cryptomining network's 320,000 RTX 3090-class GPUs allegedly burn 112 megawatts of power on ‘zero useful AI computation’ — GPU rental costs jump 38%, but Pearl’s cards are doing random matrix math, study claims
history.description: A preprint claims Pearl’s AI mining network consumes 320,000 GPU-equivalents and 112 MW while producing no verified useful AI computation.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A new research preprint claims that Pearl, a Layer-1 blockchain marketed as turning cryptocurrency mining into useful artificial intelligence (AI) computation, is doing nothing of the sort, despite having recently triggered a GPU mining rush. The study estimates Pearl's network runs at roughly 24 exahashes per second (EH/s) — the equivalent of about 320,000 RTX 3090-class GPUs drawing an estimated 112 megawatts (MW) — all while producing "zero useful AI computation." Researchers saw a roughly 38% jump in budget GPU rental prices on the marketplace vast.ai go to Pearl mining, with utilization climbing from 57% to 94% after the mining software went public in May.Instead of performing real inference or training, the paper, titled The Usefulness Gap in Proof-of-Useful-Work, says the network grinds through random matrix multiplications that merely take the shape of AI math. The self-billed “first empirical measurement of a deployed Proof-of-Useful-Work (PoUW) system” study argues that Pearl’s mining protocol verifies that miners performed matrix multiplication correctly, but does not verify whether that work came from real AI training or inference workloads.Pearl swaps Bitcoin's SHA-256 hashing for a scheme it calls cuPOW, which asks miners to compute noised integer matrix multiplications and prove they did so correctly. That operation is the same arithmetic that underpins neural network inference and training, which is the foundation of Pearl's pitch that mining and AI compute can be one and the same job. The problem, according to the study, is that the protocol's verification step only confirms that the multiplication was performed correctly. It never checks whether the input matrices came from a real model, a paying customer, or any AI workload at all.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: To demonstrate that gap, the researcher, Abhinaba Basu, built a miner that feeds the network uniformly random matrices with no inference attached, then submitted the output to a mining pool. The paper reports 44 pool-accepted shares on Nvidia and AMD hardware, with the same miner also benchmarked on a CPU and Apple Silicon, plus an on-chain payout earned by running the standard mining software unmodified. If random numbers collect rewards as readily as genuine AI work, the argument runs, then the network cannot tell the two apart, and miners have every incentive to skip the AI part entirely.Basu also analyzed 8,012 workers in a single pool, representing about 21% of Pearl's hashrate, and found that all of them ran hardware capable of AI inference. Yet, the dominant mining binary contained no identifiable code for any machine-learning framework. That binary analysis relies on string inspection, which the paper notes can be defeated by stripped or obfuscated code, so the finding is offered as strong evidence rather than outright proof. Runtime profiling pointed the same way, with the miners showing heavy compute use and light memory-bandwidth use, a signature consistent with pure matrix math and inconsistent with the memory-hungry behavior of transformer inference. You may like New AI-compute cryptocurrency Pearl sparks a GPU mining rush but profitability is already sliding Iran conflict spurs Bitcoin mining operators to pivot to AI infrastructure Tests show $30,000 AI GPUs are terrible password crackers — RTX 5090 gaming GPU outperforms Nvidia H200 and AMD MI300X For GPU buyers, the resource angle is the part that stings. The study attributes a roughly 38% jump in budget GPU rental prices on the marketplace vast.ai to Pearl mining, with utilization climbing from 57% to 94% after the mining software went public in May. Using a difference-in-differences comparison against pricier datacenter cards, Basu estimates around $600,000 per year in additional rental costs borne by independent researchers who compete for the same cheap hardware. However, he cautions that the figure depends on assumptions about how stable prices were beforehand. At PRL's recent price near $0.76, the paper calculates that mining is marginally profitable on budget cards such as the RTX 3060 Ti and roughly breakeven on an RTX 3090.The work also chips away at the assumption that Pearl mining is an Nvidia-only affair. Basu reports the first Pearl shares ever mined on non-Nvidia hardware, driving an AMD Instinct MI300X at 10.6 million tiles per second, faster than the closed-source Nvidia miner managed on an RTX 3090, and benchmarking the same workload on a server CPU and on an Apple M2 through Metal compute shaders. Because the computation is commodity integer arithmetic, the paper argues there is no vendor lock-in and no technical reason for the work to remain on any one company's silicon.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsPearl may have a ready response, but the study takes it on directly. Together AI, which announced an exclusive partnership in May, framed the deal as letting "every GPU cycle powering AI training and inference" also mint the PRL token, and it now offers a discounted Gemma-4-31B-it-pearl inference endpoint subsidized by mining proceeds. Basu counters that this is financial arbitrage rather than useful mining, because Together AI's own GPUs perform that inference separately from the mining network, with PRL revenue used to trim the endpoint's price. The 8,012 mining workers he measured, he says, produced none of that inference themselves.The study’s conclusion is not that Proof-of-Useful-Work is impossible, but that Pearl’s current design leaves a major enforcement gap. The protocol enables useful work in theory, but it does not require it in practice. That leaves Pearl in an uncomfortable middle ground — it performs real computation, but according to the paper, the network currently has no way to prove that the computation is useful AI work rather than cryptomining with AI-shaped math. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: AI加密货币挖矿网络32万块RTX 3090级GPU据称消耗112兆瓦电力，用于“零有效AI计算”——GPU租赁成本上涨38%，但研究声称Pearl的显卡在进行随机矩阵运算
history.descriptionZh:
一项最新研究预印本声称，名为Pearl的Layer-1区块链——其营销口号是将加密货币挖矿转化为有用的人工智能（AI）计算——实际上并未实现这一目标，尽管它近期引发了GPU挖矿热潮。这项研究估算，Pearl网络的算力约为每秒24 exahash（EH/s），相当于约32万张RTX 3090级别的GPU，消耗约112兆瓦电力，但“产生的有用AI计算为零”。研究人员观察到，自5月挖矿软件公开后，算力市场vast.ai上的廉价GPU租赁价格上涨了约38%，利用率从57%攀升至94%。

这篇题为《有用工作量证明的实用性鸿沟》的论文指出，Pearl网络并非执行真正的推理或训练，而是在进行随机矩阵乘法，这种运算仅仅具有AI数学的形式。作为“首个对已部署的有用工作量证明（PoUW）系统进行的实证测量”研究，该论文认为，Pearl的挖矿协议验证了矿工正确执行了矩阵乘法，但并未验证这些工作是否来自真实的AI训练或推理负载。Pearl用其名为cuPOW的方案取代了比特币的SHA-256哈希算法，该方案要求矿工计算带噪声的整数矩阵乘法，并证明其计算正确。这种运算正是神经网络推理和训练的底层算术——这也是Pearl声称挖矿与AI计算可以合二为一的基础。然而，问题在于协议验证步骤只确认了乘法是否被正确执行，它从不检查输入矩阵是否来自真实模型、付费客户或任何AI负载。

为证明这一缺口，研究人员Abhinaba Basu构建了一个矿工程序，向网络输入均匀随机矩阵（不附带任何推理），然后将输出提交至矿池。论文报告称，该矿工在NVIDIA和AMD硬件上获得了44个矿池接受的份额，并在CPU和Apple Silicon上进行了相同负载的基准测试，同时还通过运行未经修改的标准挖矿软件获得了链上奖励。论文的核心论点是：如果随机数也能像真实的AI工作一样轻易获得奖励，那么网络就无法区分二者，矿工便有充分的动机完全跳过AI部分。

Basu还分析了单个矿池中的8,012个工人（代表Pearl约21%的算力），发现所有工人都具备运行AI推理的硬件能力，但主流挖矿二进制文件中不含任何可识别的机器学习框架代码。该二进制分析依赖于字符串检查，论文指出这可能会被剥离或混淆的代码绕过，因此该发现被视为强有力的证据而非确凿证明。运行时分析也指向同一结论：矿工程序表现出高计算负载和低内存带宽使用，这与纯矩阵运算的特征一致，而与传统Transformer推理的内存密集型行为不符。

对GPU购买者而言，资源层面的影响最为刺痛。研究将vast.ai上廉价GPU租赁价格约38%的上涨归因于Pearl挖矿，自5月挖矿软件公开后，利用率从57%升至94%。通过将更昂贵的数据中心显卡进行双重差分对比，Basu估计独立研究人员每年额外承担约60万美元的租赁成本——不过他也谨慎指出，该数字依赖于对先前价格稳定性的假设。以PRL近期约0.76美元的价格计算，论文估算在RTX 3060 Ti等廉价显卡上挖矿勉强盈利，在RTX 3090上则大致持平。

该工作还打破了Pearl挖矿仅限NVIDIA硬件的假设。Basu报告了首个在非NVIDIA硬件上挖出的Pearl份额：在AMD Instinct MI300X上达到每秒1060万tiles，超过了闭源NVIDIA矿工在RTX 3090上的表现；同时在同一负载下对服务器CPU和Apple M2（通过Metal计算着色器）进行了基准测试。由于计算本身是通用整数算术，论文认为不存在供应商锁定，也没有技术理由将工作局限于某一家公司的芯片。

对于Pearl可能的回应，研究直接进行了反驳。Together AI在5月宣布与Pearl建立独家合作，声称该合作伙伴关系让“每一条驱动AI训练和推理的GPU周期”都能同时铸造PRL代币，并推出了一种由挖矿收益补贴的打折Gemma-4-31B-it-pearl推理端点。Basu反驳称，这本质上是金融套利而非有用挖矿，因为Together AI自己的GPU独立于挖矿网络执行推理，只是用PRL收入来降低端点价格。他测量的那8,012个挖矿工人，没有产生任何推理工作。

研究的结论并非“有用工作量证明”不可能实现，而是Pearl当前的设计存在重大执行缺口。该协议在理论上支持有用工作，但在实践中并未强制要求。这使得Pearl陷入了一个尴尬的中间地带——它执行了真实计算，但根据论文，该网络目前无法证明这些计算是有用的AI工作，而非只是披着AI数学外衣的加密货币挖矿。
